commit.message:
fix(electron): use CommonJS preload to resolve ESM-in-ASAR error

Preload scripts cannot use ESM import syntax when loaded from inside an
ASAR archive with "type":"module" in the root package.json. Renamed
preload.js → preload.cjs and switched to require() to fix the
'SyntaxError: Cannot use import statement outside a module' error that
prevented window.electronAPI from being exposed to the renderer.

commit.message:
sh: Fix linker errors by applying __USER_LABEL_PREFIX__ in CNAME (#953)

The CNAME macro in src/sh/sysv.S was defined as a no-op (#define CNAME(x) x),
which ignores __USER_LABEL_PREFIX__. On targets where this prefix is set (e.g.
an underscore on some platforms), the assembler emits symbols without the
expected prefix, causing linker errors when C code references them.

Fix this by using the C1/C2 two-level macro concatenation pattern (as used in
x86/sysv.S) to properly prepend __USER_LABEL_PREFIX__ to symbol names.

This replaces the approach proposed in #809.

Co-authored-by: bobo215 <266481280+bobo215@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix microphone permission & detection flow for iOS 26

- Replace permission_handler_apple (AVCaptureDevice) with a native
  AVAudioSession method channel on iOS — AVCaptureDevice.requestAccessForMediaType(.audio)
  silently kills the app on iOS 26 simulator
- Disable mic_stream's internal permission_handler call via
  MicStream.shouldRequestPermission(false) to prevent the same crash
- Restore audioFormat: ENCODING_PCM_16BIT in MicStream.microphone() —
  mic_stream's default is 8-bit which iOS explicitly rejects
- Add PitchServiceError typed error so the provider can distinguish
  permission failures from mic hardware/API failures
- Add micError state field to TunerState and a dismissible amber banner
  in TunerScreen for non-permission mic failures
- Add checkPermission() for read-only status checks in the error handler

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Prefer system ffmpeg over ffmpeg-static bundled binary

ffmpeg-static returns a path without throwing, but the binary
doesn't work on Railway's Linux. Now checks if system ffmpeg
is available first (installed via NIXPACKS_PKGS) and only falls
back to ffmpeg-static if not.

commit.message:
refactor: replace remaining-questions with answered-questions in LLM prompt

Instead of listing every unanswered question in the user message sent to
the LLM, now pass only the IDs of questions already answered (derived
from currentScores). This keeps the prompt focused on what has been
covered rather than pre-exposing what's coming.

Changes:
- buildSingleLlmUserMessage: "Remaining unanswered questions:" →
  "Answered questions: Q1, Q2, ..." (comma-separated IDs from currentScores)
- buildSingleLlmVariables: 'remaining' variable renamed to 'answered'
  and now holds the answered question IDs in the same format.

Note: the OpenAI stored prompt must be updated to use {{answered}}
instead of {{remaining}} for this variable slot.

commit.message:
fix: mobile responsive audit — 3 overflow bugs corregidos

- html overflow-x: hidden — fix root cause: cuando html tiene overflow:visible
  y body overflow:hidden, los position:fixed con right:0 se anclan al
  scrollable width (478px) en lugar del viewport (390px). Afectaba promo-bar
  y scroll-progress en todas las páginas. También previene que transforms
  AOS/v7 causen horizontal scroll en el body.

- compare-table-wrap — planes.html: tabla 700px con min-width causaba
  scrollWidth:474px. Fix: width:100%+display:block en @media ≤768px para
  que overflow:auto cree scroll container correcto (700px table scrollable
  dentro del wrapper, página no scrollable horizontalmente).

- blog nav MI CUENTA — selector v6.css usa `>` (hijo directo) pero en blog
  pages el botón está dentro de un div extra. Override con descendant
  selector + white-space:nowrap. nav-cta oculto en ≤991px (daba 2 elementos
  en navbar mobile causando wrap).

Verificado en Chrome mobile 390x844 (iPhone 14 Pro):
  index ✅ planes ✅ rise ✅ tienda ✅ metodo ✅ blog ✅ coaches ✅

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add external configs tracking and fix Qt/KDE theming

- configs/: new directory tracking all configs deployed outside the
  quickshell dir, so the future install script has a single source
  of truth:
  - matugen/config.toml — full matugen config with kdeglobals templates
  - matugen/templates/kde/kdeglobals — matugen template generating
    ~/.config/kdeglobals and ~/.local/share/color-schemes/CdlNiri.colors
  - matugen/templates/kde/color.txt — source color extractor template
  - matugen/templates/kde/kde-material-you-colors-wrapper.sh — legacy
    wrapper (kept for reference, superseded by direct matugen template)
  - xdg-desktop-portal/niri-portals.conf — uses kde;gtk portals for
    correct dark-mode detection on niri
  - environment.d/cdl-niri.conf — QT_QPA_PLATFORMTHEME=kde,
    ELECTRON_OZONE_PLATFORM_HINT=auto, TERMINAL=ghostty
  - niri/cdl/binds.kdl — all cdl-niri keybinds for niri compositor
  - niri/cdl/auto-expand.py — auto-expand script spawned by niri
  - niri/config-additions.kdl — reference snippet for install script
    to merge into user's niri/config.kdl

- switchwall.sh: replace broken kde-material-you-colors call with
  notify_qt_color_change() that touches kdeglobals/CdlNiri.colors
  after matugen already generated them, triggering inotify reload in
  running KDE/Qt apps

Cache Infisical auth token with retry-on-401 pattern

Replaces per-call re-authentication with a cached `isAuthenticated` flag.
The client authenticates once and reuses the token indefinitely, re-authenticating
only when a 401 Unauthorized response is received.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix parts finder: navigate to search results context, not homepage

Go to /?rf=kw&kw=vehicle-fitment&templatebody=parts-finder-results&fitsku=...
instead of /?templatebody=parts-finder-results&fitsku=...

Homepage context was loading carousels + featured products before our template,
causing 30+ second load times. Search results context (rf=kw) skips all of that.
The parts-finder-results template uses filter_sku which overrides the kw filter.
Results page has full sidebar with category/attribute/price/brand filters.

Also add rf+kw hidden inputs to sort form so sort changes stay in search context.

commit.message:
Fix standard claims not matched due to Micronaut kebab-case normalization

Micronaut's @EachProperty normalizes YAML keys to kebab-case (e.g.
preferred_username becomes preferred-username), causing standard OpenID
claims with underscores in their IDs to be misidentified as custom claims.
